_meta.description: M&A status registry for watchlist tickers. Drives ACQ/BID/RUMOR pills + deal blurbs in drilldown popup, replacing the earnings note slot. Only tickers with verified Tier-1 sourcing (SEC filings, issuer press releases) reach status='closed' or 'pending'. Rumors require 4 gates (see schema).
rumor_gates: Story names specific buyer or transaction
Story published before or coincident with the move

CFLT.strategic_rationale: Acquires Kafka franchise as the real-time data backbone for IBM's enterprise AI/agent platform. Confluent slots beside Red Hat in IBM Software, giving IBM control of streaming infrastructure underneath gen-AI workloads.
pros: All-cash deal at $31/share — clean exit, no IBM equity risk
Buyer is investment-grade — closing risk near zero post-announce
cons: Locks Confluent into IBM's GTM motion; cloud-native customers may slow Kafka spend pending integration clarity
Loss of pure-play streaming public comp removes a key data-infrastructure reference

competitor_impact.note: Azure Event Hubs sees less competitive pressure from a hyperscaler-neutral Confluent; modest tailwind
Loses an independent partner; Snowflake-Confluent integrations now run through IBM's roadmap priorities
Observability of Kafka streams becomes more strategic as IBM consolidates the layer

OS.strategic_rationale: Hg take-private with General Atlantic and Tidemark as minority co-investors. KKR (prior majority shareholder) approved as voting majority; no separate shareholder vote required. Restores OneStream as a long-duration sponsor-backed CPM platform free from quarterly print pressure.
pros: Hg is a deep CPM/finance-software specialist (Visma, IRIS) — operational fit is high
Closing risk minimized by KKR's pre-committed approval
cons: Removes a scarce mid-cap CPM pure-play from the public comp set
Take-private cuts off public-disclosure visibility into CPM growth + retention trends
Hg-led leverage profile may tighten reinvestment vs. prior public model

competitor_impact.note: Adaptive Planning loses an independent comparable; pricing power on CPM modestly stronger
Oracle EPM Cloud removes a key public-market scoreboard reference
SAP Group Reporting / Datasphere face a private, patient OneStream rather than a quarterly-print competitor — net neutral

sources.label: OneStream — deal closing press release (PR Newswire)
